authoritative_sources.title: Rules on mortgage servicing
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-financial regulator for mortgage servicing. This official rules hub identifies the governing Regulation X and Regulation Z provisions and links the current official interpretations and examination materials used to supervise servicers.
used_for: linking servicing subworkflows to the governing regulations
tracking active versus proposed mortgage-servicing rule changes

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.35 Procedures to resolve errors
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Regulation X rule text for borrower notices of error, required investigation, response timing, and limited refusal grounds in mortgage servicing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Borrower inquiry, complaint, and servicing-error handling

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.36 Requests for information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Regulation X rule text for information-request intake, acknowledgments, time limits, owner-information requests, and protected refusal grounds.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Borrower information requests and owner-information handling
used_for: owner or assignee information response timing
limits on refusing overbroad or privileged requests

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.38 General servicing policies, procedures, and requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Regulation X rule text for servicing-file controls, loss-mitigation support procedures, service-provider oversight, and transfer-of-information expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence handling, transfers, and system-of-record governance

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1024.41 Loss mitigation procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Regulation X rule text governing complete-application handling, evaluation timing, appeal rights, and foreclosure-referral restrictions in mortgage loss mitigation.

authoritative_sources.title: 12 CFR 1026.41 Periodic statements for residential mortgage loans
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Regulation Z rule text governing statement content, transaction activity, partial-payment disclosures, and delinquency information for residential mortgage loans.

authoritative_sources.title: Mortgage servicing examination procedures
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CFPB supervisory procedures that operationalize the mortgage servicing rules into examination modules covering routine servicing, default servicing, and foreclosure.
used_for: separating routine servicing from default servicing and foreclosure
mapping scenario families to supervisory risk areas

authoritative_sources.title: A4-1-03, Addressing Borrower Inquiries and Disputes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicing guide content for Fannie-owned or securitized loans. It is authoritative when those investor rules apply and directly addresses borrower inquiries, dispute handling, and caution around foreclosure during disputes.

authoritative_sources.title: C-1.1-01, Servicer Responsibilities for Processing Mortgage Loan Payments
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicing guide content for Fannie-owned or securitized loans that governs payment crediting, application order, custodial-account deposits, and payment-change notifications.
used_for: custodial deposit timing and borrower-notice language

authoritative_sources.title: C-1.1-02, Processing Payment Shortages or Funds Received When a Mortgage Loan Modification Is Pending
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicing guide content for handling payment shortages, unapplied funds, late charges, and pending-modification funds when Fannie investor rules govern the loan.

authoritative_sources.title: D2-2-05, Receiving a Borrower Response Package
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicing guide content for complete and incomplete borrower response packages, notice requirements, evaluation timing, and response windows for Fannie-owned or securitized loans.

authoritative_sources.title: F-1-03, Establishing and Implementing Custodial Accounts
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official servicing guide procedure defining custodial-account setup, daily cut-off discipline, and custodial reconciliation requirements for Fannie-owned or securitized loans.

authoritative_sources.title: Suspicious Activity Reporting Overview
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency BSA/AML examination-manual content used by banking regulators to assess suspicious-activity controls and confidentiality.
used_for: ownership shift from servicing to AML or fraud

authoritative_sources.title: Appendix P: BSA Record Retention Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FFIEC appendix summarizing BSA retention requirements, including the five-year retention period for SARs and supporting documentation.
used_for: refusal to discard or suppress suspicious-activity evidence

workflow_stages.name: Servicing intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the request is a supported loan-servicing issue, determine the governing product, jurisdiction, investor or program, and classify whether the work stays in ordinary servicing support or immediately shifts to an adjacent owner.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Payment, escrow, and account-truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Compare loan-servicing, payment, escrow, statement, suspense, and custodial records to determine whether authoritative evidence supports the claimed account status or whether reconciliation is required.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Borrower inquiry, notice-of-error, and information-request handling
workflow_stages.description: Validate that borrower inquiry or error allegations contain sufficient identifiers, follow the required intake channel and timing rules, and produce evidence-backed response or blocked-state outputs.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Borrower communication and periodic-statement support
workflow_stages.description: Use current statement, delinquency, transaction-activity, and communication records to explain the servicing state without overstating finality or bypassing required disclosures.

workflow_stages.name: Delinquency and loss-mitigation support
workflow_stages.description: Confirm whether the file reflects early-intervention, continuity, complete-package, evaluation, appeal, and foreclosure-referral controls, then route or summarize the next step without promising an unapproved workout outcome.
source_classes: Public servicing, payments, or policy workflow rules

workflow_stages.name: Fraud, AML, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop in-lane handling and escalate when identity theft, suspicious payment activity, consumer-harm risk, legal interpretation, bankruptcy, foreclosure, or approval authority materially changes the safe answer.

artifacts: loan-servicing system records including note terms, due dates, payment history, delinquency buckets, and servicing notes
escrow ledgers, suspense or unapplied-funds balances, lockbox or ACH records, custodial-account evidence, and periodic statements
notices of error, requests for information, borrower complaints, payoff requests, owner-information requests, and communication logs
loss-mitigation artifacts including BRPs, hardship documentation, incomplete-package notices, evaluation notices, appeal status, and foreclosure milestones
fraud, AML, identity-theft, bankruptcy, SCRA, approval-history, and exception-routing artifacts
tenant policy extracts, investor or guarantor overlays, product-specific procedures, and jurisdiction-specific servicing rules
systems_of_record: lockbox, ACH, autopay, or payment processor platform
CRM, complaint management, and case management systems
foreclosure, bankruptcy, and default-servicing tracking systems
fraud monitoring and AML case systems

allowed: Validate whether retrieved records support a documented payment, escrow, delinquency, statement, inquiry, or loss-mitigation status.
Prepare traceable summaries, communication notes, and escalation packets with explicit uncertainty and owner routing.
Request the minimum additional loan, payment, investor, or tenant policy facts needed to continue safely.
escalate: Investor, guarantor, product, or jurisdiction context is missing and would change the borrower-impacting result
Payment, statement, escrow, delinquency, or foreclosure records disagree
Fraud, AML, identity-theft, suspicious payment activity, or consumer-harm signals are present
Modification approval, foreclosure hold, bankruptcy handling, or legal interpretation is required
refuse: Requests to alter loan status, payment application, fees, payoff, or modification outcome without source evidence and approval ownership
Requests for regulated advice, legal interpretation, underwriting, foreclosure counsel decisions, or final credit-reporting outcomes
Requests to treat mortgage-specific public rules as automatically applicable to other loan products without retrieved tenant context

domain_failure_modes: Telling a borrower the loan is current, approved, or foreclosure-safe when the authoritative servicing, statement, and default records do not agree
Applying mortgage-servicing timelines or investor-specific rules to the wrong product, investor, or jurisdiction
Reallocating or re-aging payments, escrow, or suspense balances without documented evidence and approval authority
Proceeding with ordinary servicing communication when fraud, AML, or suspicious-activity handling should take over
Collapsing loan-servicing terminology into generic support language and dropping key artifacts such as BRPs, periodic statements, payoff quotes, custodial accounts, or delinquency notices

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Disclosure or reconciliation conflict that requires escalation

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Generic loan-servicing versus mortgage-specific public rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The strongest public servicing baseline is mortgage focused. For auto, student, or unsecured consumer loan servicing, the pack requires tenant product context and refuses confident borrower-impacting synthesis when equivalent product-specific rules are not retrieved.

Fannie Mae public servicing rules are authoritative only for loans they own or securitize. The pack uses those sources to constrain workflow design, but final borrower-impacting handling still depends on retrieved investor or guarantor mapping.

The CFPB mortgage servicing rules hub still referenced a July 10, 2024 proposed rule at the time of review. The pack relies on currently effective regulations and treats any unadopted proposal as non-operative until finalized.
